title: Economics Foundations
description: This covers virtually every core undergraduate economics topic without becoming overly specialized.

title: Microeconomics
description: This course focuses on **how individuals and firms make economic decisions**.

title: Macroeconomics
description: This course explains **how entire economies function**, including growth, inflation, employment, and government policy.

title: Consumer Theory
description: This course explores **how consumers make choices to maximize satisfaction under limited resources.**

title: Producer Theory
description: This course explains **how firms transform resources into goods and services while maximizing profits.**

title: Market Structures
description: This course explains **how different market structures influence pricing, competition, efficiency, and firm behavior.**

title: International Economics
description: This course explains **why nations trade, how global markets operate, and how international policies shape the world economy.**

title: Public Economics
description: This course explores **how governments influence economic outcomes through taxation, spending, regulation, and public policy.**

title: Monetary Economics
description: This course explains **how money is created, how financial systems operate, and how central banks influence economic activity.**

title: Development Economics
description: This course examines **why some countries become prosperous while others remain underdeveloped, and the policies that promote long-term development.**

title: Behavioral Economics
description: This course explores **how real people make economic decisions, often deviating from the predictions of traditional economic theory.**

title: Econometrics
description: This is the capstone course of the Economics curriculum, teaching students **how economists use data and statistical methods to test theories and make evidence-based decisions.**
